rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/1/2021. 1)9/13/2021 filing was not responsive to this issue. Continued again for same issue. Please provide legal authority/probate code sections in support of the requests in the petition and explain how the legal authority/probate code sections apply. There appears to be no legal authority for the request for a second preliminary distribution. 2) 9/13/2021 filing was not responsive to this issue. Continue again for same issue. Please explain provide a legal basis for the court issuing an order "authorizing petitioner to complete the settlement with the former tenants of estate real property by paying the sum of $125,000.00 to such tenants" when there is already a 1/30/2020 court order that says "administrator is authorized to pay the balance of the settlement amount ($125,000.00) to defendants pursuant to the modified stipulation for entry od judgment upon close of escrow for sale of 61 capp street by the estate. 3) Issue for eventual hearing: whether to order a 2-year account under Probate Code 10950. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR CADIZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/29/21. (No earlier availability.) Now a FIFTH Amended First And Final Account, etc., has been filed. No proof of service has been filed. The amended petition and account have not been fully reviewed yet; however, it was noted that the accounting now begins with only income receipts, not the total assets shown in the two I&As, which totaled $695,994.42. It is also noted that paragraph 2 of this now fifth amended petition does not mention the codicil dated 1/20/2016 that was also admitted on 3/21/2017, when the executor was appointed, not 4/24/2017, when she obtained her Letters. The initiating petition filed by Joseph Cadiz was filed on 7/15/2016, not 8/10/2016. The Partial No. One I&A filed on 8/25/2017 showed all assets as "cash" when they clearly were not. The Final I&A filed on 4/9/2018 showed assets totaling $261,287.66. It is unclear what the value of the assets and what the assets were as of the decedent's date of death. Should the Final I&A have been a Corrected Partial No. One and Final I&A? There is no "Exhibit G (Schedule B)" listing claims. There is no Probate Code Section 9292. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or BENEFICIARY has PREVIOUSLY BEEN confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (The first paragraph 8 of the fifth amended petition does not fully address this Code section either way.) Paragraph 11 is meant to address "personal property taxes", but addresses estate taxes? Now, the executor claims that there are no state of federal income taxes due or payable by the estate? The character of property is either separate property or community property No calculation for the proposed distribution is shown. If there was $202,526.06 on hand at "present" (which is not a date certain), how did the executor arrive at a total of $202,508.74? The accounting (presumably Exhibit G) is NOT a proper Court conformed accounting. Counsel apparently still has not reviewed Probate Code Section 1060 et seq., including 1062. Again, if claims have not been paid, the creditor must also be given notice.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/01/2021. 1) Please provide a legal basis/cite legal authority for the conservator not complying with the 8/26/2019 Order that states, "Petitioner shall reimburse the estate $475.00 for reimbursements made from the estate without substantiation." 2) Please provide a legal basis/cite legal authority for the court awarding attorney fees for correcting the inventory and appraisal ("I&A") that did not list the correct assets. It appears $635.00 was spent on correcting the I&A that should be deducted from the attorney fee request. See attorney fee entries 4/2/2019, 4/3/2019 and 4/4/2019. 3) Please file receipts for entries 10/4/2019, 12/12/2019, 12/31/2019 Home Care Assistance Invoices. 4) Please file receipts and explain the $4,382.20 in fees spend on preparing an account in this account as this is unusually high. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/18/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ROWAN STRUMMER TELLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/2021. Issues (to be addressed before hearing): 1) Account period ends 01/24/2021. Letter from morgan stanley alleges as on 01/24/2020 the account is valued at $250,383.64. Please file bank statements that verify the balance of the account at the end of the account period 01/24/2021. 2) If over $250,000.00 is in a single account please provide legal authority/legal basis/cite probate code sections for keeping cash over the $250k FDIC limit in a single account. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) This petition covers account period 01/25/2019 to 01/24/2021. No probate code or legal authority cited for requesting reimbursement for the time period prior to 01/25/2019 or after 01/24/2021. Counsel should be prepared at the hearing to cite probate code sections and other legal authority in support of the request. Guardian is requesting 7 year old ward reimburse for the following: a. $12,152.00 for HOA - No legal authority or probate code sections cited for a legal basis for why 7-year-old ward should pay the cost of a $75.00 FOB that occurred in the prior account period, and $25.00 for a storage room key. It appears only $9,335.025 should be reimbursed for the HOA fees during this the account period covered by this account petition. a. $8,438.00 in property taxes. It appears only $6,634.23 in city and county of SF property taxes were paid during this account period. ½ is $3,317.115. b. $6,441.00 in state/fed taxes. Canceled checks appear to total $6,441.00. ½ is $3,220.50. 2) Supplement filed 08/06/2021 amends the petition and should have been filed as an amendment or amended petition. Notice of the Supplement was given 8/6/2021. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 10/12/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GARY K. CARUSO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgment For Order To Establish, Join And Fund An Account For Conservatee In The California Chrities Pooled Trust
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests authority to transfer to the Pooled SNT the proceeds of on the sale of real property, less various expenses. Court to determine whether a specific amount is needed. 2) Petitioner alleges that the amount of bond is not known, as it was unclear how much would be received on sale of real property. 3) The terms of the trust allow for a "Directed Trustee" under Section 2.3 and a "Designated MSA Vendor" under Section 2.7 to be appointed. Petitioner alleges that neither is expected to be appointed. 4) Petitioner states that there is no trust provision that requires court approval of changes in trustees or a court order appointing any successor trustee, as required under CRC 7.903(c)(7). Petitioner requests that the order approving the petition include such language. 5) Court to determine whether all proposed fees and expenses are reasonable. 6) Court to determine whether proposed distribution upon termination under the master agreement is acceptable. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER SOO HOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/21. (No earlier availability.) The "Supplement" filed on 8/24/21 amended the amended petition. [CRC 7.3.] Prob. C. Sect. 12001 states that the rate of interest is three percentage points less than the legal rate on judgments. In other words, interest on pecuniary bequests not made within one year from the date of death accrue 7% interest or, in this estate, $1,050/year or $2.88/day. As of 7/31/21, that would have been 237 days, or $682.56 to each pecuniary devisee. Ordinarily, when the fee base is calculated using any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales are required to be filed (CRC 7.705(b)]; however, the "Reduction in Inventory" on page 2 of the "Supplement" filed on 8/24/21 (without any description) will be accepted without a detailed schedule, as it appears to be for the tangible personal property mentioned on page 6 (without a value) as having been donated to charity. The "carry values" shown on page 2 of the "Supplement" are not, in fact, the carry values (I&A values). They appear to be the fair market values. In any event, the value of securities is not necessary for the proposed distribution; only the number of shares of each security, as values fluctuate. The executor never petitioned to increase bond even after the additional savings account was discovered. [CRC 7.204(a).] An amendment to the amended petition must be filed. Notice of the amendment to the amended petition must be given after the amendment is E-filed. Courtesy copies of the amendment to the amended petition and the proof of service and a revised proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD A. TOLENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To For Relief From Untimely Filing Of Will Contest And Petition To Revoke Probate Of Purported Will (Code Civ. Proc., 473(B).)
rulings.Rulings: [3 of 3] Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 28, 2021. Respondent Ronald M. Tolentino is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative prior to the time set for this hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD A. TOLENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To For Relief From Untimely Filing Of Will Contest And Petition To Revoke Probate Of Purported Will (Code Civ. Proc., 473(B).) (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 2] Regarding the request for relief under Probate Code Section 8007(b), "extrinsic fraud" under that section requires more than a showing of intrinsic fraud in the original action, or a "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect" described by Code of Civil Procedure Section 437. The kind of "fraud" which could potentially except a will contest from the 120-day statutory limitation must be sufficient to deprive the complaining party of a fair opportunity to fully present their case, "by fraud or deception practiced by an opponent, as by keeping the party away from court, a false, promise of a compromise; or where the defendant never had knowledge of the suit, being kept in ignorance by the acts of the plaintiff; or where an attorney fraudulently or without authority assumes to represent a party and connives at the party's defeat; or where the attorney regularly employed corruptly sells out the client's interest to the other side, these, and similar cases which show that there has never been a real contest in the trial or hearing of the case [. . .]." (Estate of Sanders (1985) 40 Ca.3d 607, 614, quoting United States v. Throckmorton (1898) 98 U.S. 61, 65-66; see also Estate of Beard (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 753, 772-775.) Contestant has failed to make a showing of extrinsic fraud sufficient to grant relief under Probate Code section 8007. To obtain equitable relief based on extrinsic mistake, a party has the burden to show three different elements. "'First, the defaulted party must demonstrate that it has a meritorious case. Second [], the party seeking to set aside the default must articulate a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action. Last[], the moving party must demonstrate diligence in seeking to set aside the default once . . . discovered.' [Citation.]" (Cruz v. Fagor Am., Inc. (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 488, 503; see also Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co, Inc. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 736; Estate of Beard, supra, 71 Cal.App.4th at pp. 774-776; Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 855-866; Kulchar v. Kulchar (1969) 1 Cal.3d 467, 470-473.) Contestant has not met this burden.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD A. TOLENTINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion To For Relief From Untimely Filing Of Will Contest And Petition To Revoke Probate Of Purported Will (Code Civ. Proc., 473(B).) (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody Contestant's request for judicial notice is DENIED. The documents requested to be judicially noticed do not fall into any category of matters that may be judicially noticed under Evidence Code Sections 451 and 452. Contestant's Motion to for Relief from Untimely Filing of Will Contest and Petition to Revoke Probate of Purported Will is DENIED. Neither the mandatory nor the discretionary provisions of Code of Civil Procedure Section 473 apply. The order admitting the will to probate and appointing the executor is not a "judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against" the Contestant or a "resulting default entered by the clerk against [Contestant], and which will result in entry of a default judgment, or (2) resulting default judgment or dismissal entered against [Contestant]." Furthermore, the deadline set forth in Probate Code Section 8270(a) is jurisdictional (See Estate of Harootenian (1951) 38 Cal.2d 242, 246; see also Probate Code Section 8007) and Code of Civil Procedure Section 473 does not afford relief from a deadline that is jurisdictional. (See Maynard v. Brandon (2005) 36 Cal.4th 364, 372.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WYMAN SETO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/21 . (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Notice of the amended petition was not given to all interested parties. [Prob. C. Sect. 8110.] A petitioner does not need to give notice to herself. [Prob. C. Sect. 1201.] Notice must be of the AMENDED Petition, not an "amended notice", etc. (The proof of service filed on 9/7/21 and the proof of publication filed on 9/14/21 both show that an Amended Notice was served of the Petition for Probate, not a notice of an Amended Petition for Probate.) (Please do not attach proofs of service to pleadings filed in Probate; that may be acceptable in Civil cases , but all Probate notices must be on the mandatory Judicial Council forms, the reverse sides of which are the proofs of service that should be completed.) A proposed personal representative must sign and verify any supplement or amendment to her own pleadings. [CRC 7.103(c).] Nothing has been filed yet to indicate who the person is who has legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/10/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GRANDVEL ANDREW JACKSON IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Co-Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. Issues: The Court to determine if Lura Sims and Mary Alice Oliver should be appointed co-trustees of the Grandvel Andrew Jackson Irrevocable Trust B without bond, if the agreement they entered into in June 2021 as successor co-trustees should be approved, and if Lura Sims should be authorized to reimburse herself $17,897.00 in attorney fees she reportedly incurred to "pursue" this petition. 1) This Court's jurisdiction over this trust was terminated on 8/28/2006 but the petition does not address this. Furthermore, both proposed successor co-trustees reside in Texas. They seek appointment here because the trust was originally brought under Court supervision during the lifetime of the surviving trustor, Cora Mary Sutton Jackson, after subtrust funding was authorized by substituted judgment in her conservatorship case. Does the Court accept that venue is proper here, notwithstanding the unfortunate language in the 2006 order terminating this Court's jurisdiction of this trust? 2) The petition caption fails to disclose that the petitioner is also seeking approval of the agreement and reimbursement of her attorney fees. Her attorney cites the CCP but CRC 7.50 and 7.102 apply to Probate proceedings, where specific and full notice to all interested parties is critical. Does the Court wish to accept a proposed amended petition attached to an attorney declaration and without notice? 3) It is noted, however, that (a) the attorney fees are not supported by any fee declaration and (b) the proposed amended petition attached to the attorney's Declaration filed on 9/2/21 is incomplete (no exhibits). 4) Rather than set forth the correct, new address for Theodore Bennett, the attorney claims in his Declaration filed on 9/2/21 that the address on the Notice of Hearing Proof of Service is the updated address. 5) Rather than file a corrected proof of service, showing the correct address to which the notice was mailed to the NAACP, the attorney merely states in his Declaration filed on 9/2/21 that notice was given to the address shown in the petition. In any event, the gift to the NAACP was reportedly satisfied, and notice was given only out of an abundance of caution.
